findings.title: Avoid shared atomic increments on every URMA fast-path request
findings.impact: Every successful URMA Set/Get now calls RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult and lands in RecordInCurrentWindow, where all client threads contend on the same successCount_/failureCount_ cache lines. On a high-QPS multi-threaded client this adds a cross-core atomic read-modify-write to the RDMA fast path and can reduce the throughput/latency benefit that URMA is supposed to provide even when the data plane is healthy.
findings.fix: Replace the single shared counters with a lower-contention design, such as per-thread/per-shard counters that are aggregated only when the window settles, or another batched/sampled accounting scheme that avoids an atomic RMW on every healthy URMA request. Keep the failure-trigger path bounded and preserve the min-sample/window semantics.
